title: Non-record: Online Hessian GPTQ (val_bpb=1.1349)
author: ibarrajo
body:
## Summary

- **NEGATIVE RESULT**: Online Hessian GPTQ — accumulate Hessian matrix H during training to eliminate post-training GPTQ calibration
- Hypothesis: removing GPTQ time reservation frees more steps for training
- Reality: online Hessian overhead (112ms vs 95ms/step) costs more steps than it saves

## Results

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| val_bpb (base) | 1.1390 |
| val_bpb (TTT) | **1.1349** |
| Artifact size | 14.9 MB |
| Step time | 112 ms |
| Total steps | ~5,350 |
| Training time | ~600s |

## Key Findings (Negative Result)

1. **Online Hessian adds ~17ms/step overhead**: Extra computation to accumulate H during forward pass. At 5,000+ steps, this costs ~85 seconds — more than a standard 30-40s post-training GPTQ calibration.
2. **Quantization quality is comparable**: The online Hessian produces similar GPTQ quality to post-training calibration — no quality advantage to offset the speed cost.
3. **Step budget is everything**: At 95ms/step you get ~6,150 steps; at 112ms you get ~5,350. Those 800 lost training steps matter more than saving 30s of GPTQ time.

## Why Non-record

The core insight: in a time-constrained competition, any per-step overhead must justify itself in quality. Online Hessian GPTQ doesn't — it trades training steps for a negligible calibration time savings.

## Rule Compliance

- [x] Training < 600s on 8xH100
- [x] Artifact < 16,000,000 bytes (14.9MB)
- [x] No val tokens in artifact
- [x] GPTQ calibration integrated in training (within budget by design)
